perturbation.original_text: Buyer shall not contact the Seller or any other individual or entity associated with Seller or the Business including, without limitation , landlords, employees, suppliers or customers except upon the prior written consent of Seller . All correspondence, inquiries, and offers to purchase, and other documents relating to the Business or Transaction (all of which is “Confidential Information) will be delivered solely through Broker , and all negotiations relating to the Business or Transaction will be conducted exclusively through Broker.
perturbation.changed_text: Buyer shall not contact the Seller directly without the Broker's explicit written consent. However, Buyer is permitted to contact landlords and suppliers of the Business for preliminary due diligence, and such contact does not require prior consent. All correspondence, inquiries, and offers to purchase, and other documents relating to the Business or Transaction (all of which is “Confidential Information) will be delivered solely through Broker , and all negotiations relating to the Business or Transaction will be conducted exclusively through Broker.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates a contradiction by initially prohibiting any contact with the Seller or related entities without consent, but then permitting contact with landlords and suppliers for preliminary due diligence without consent. This introduces an in-text contradiction regarding whether prior consent is always required or if there are exceptions. The contradiction lies in the different rules about contacting the seller and their business contacts without consent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: DIRECT ALL CONTACT THROUGH BROKER Buyer shall not contact the Seller directly without the Broker's explicit written consent. However, Buyer is permitted to contact landlords and suppliers of the Business for preliminary due diligence, and such contact does not require prior consent. All correspondence, inquiries, and offers to purchase, and other documents relating to the Business or Transaction (all of which is “Confidential Information) will be delivered solely through Broker , and all negotiations relating to the Business or Transaction will be conducted exclusively through Broker.
perturbation.justification: The original text strictly prohibited any contact with the Seller's associated entities without prior written consent. The changed text now permits the Buyer to contact landlords and suppliers for preliminary due diligence without prior consent, directly contradicting the initial prohibition. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text so the contradiction is within itself. Therefore, the change creates a strong in-text contradiction regarding the necessity of prior consent, as it establishes exceptions to a previously absolute rule.

perturbation.original_text: All information about the Business is provided by the Seller and is not verified by Broker. Buyer understands that purchasing a ny business represents investment risks and that Buyer should obtain professional assistance from independent accounting, legal, and financial advisors to verify all information prior to consummating an agreement to purchase the Business. Buyer will not rely on the information provided by Broker or Seller , including the Confidential Information , but shall conduct its own independen t due diligence . Seller (and not Broker) is the source of all information and statements about the Business. Broker makes no warranty , guarantee, expressed or implied, as to the accuracy of such information.
perturbation.changed_text: All information about the Business is provided by the Seller and while not actively verified, Broker warrants that it is a fair representation based on the information available. Buyer understands that purchasing any business represents investment risks and that Buyer should obtain professional assistance from independent accounting, legal, and financial advisors to verify all information prior to consummating an agreement to purchase the Business. Buyer will not solely rely on the information provided by Broker or Seller , including the Confidential Information , but shall conduct its own independent due diligence . Seller (and not Broker) is the primary source of all information and statements about the Business. Broker offers a limited warranty of accuracy based on the information provided by the seller.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces a contradiction by changing the statement from 'Broker makes no warranty' to 'Broker offers a limited warranty of accuracy'. Previously, the Broker explicitly disclaimed any warranty. Now, the Broker is providing a 'limited warranty', creating an in-text contradiction regarding the Broker's responsibility for the information's accuracy. This contradicts the original statement that the information isn't verified and introduces ambiguity about the scope and nature of the 'limited warranty'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROVID ED BY SELLER All information about the Business is provided by the Seller and while not actively verified, Broker warrants that it is a fair representation based on the information available. Buyer understands that purchasing any business represents investment risks and that Buyer should obtain professional assistance from independent accounting, legal, and financial advisors to verify all information prior to consummating an agreement to purchase the Business. Buyer will not solely rely on the information provided by Broker or Seller , including the Confidential Information , but shall conduct its own independent due diligence . Seller (and not Broker) is the primary source of all information and statements about the Business. Broker offers a limited warranty of accuracy based on the information provided by the seller.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a 'limited warranty of accuracy' by the Broker, which directly contradicts the original statement in the original text and elsewhere in the document that 'Broker makes no warranty'. This is a clear inconsistency as defined in the Perturbation Definitions, creating a strong in-text contradiction regarding the Broker's responsibility for the information's accuracy. The contradicted text also has the same contradiction in the same place, which demonstrates that the contradiction exists. The original text stated 'Broker makes no warranty , guarantee, expressed or implied, as to the accuracy of such information,' while the changed text states 'Broker offers a limited warranty of accuracy based on the information provided by the seller.'
